deductions: Agent did not produce make grade results

eval_report.comment: Passing: Some fix work done, but verification insufficient or report quality lacking.

IV. Bug Report Quality (20 pts).problem_description: 4/6 — Describes build failures and points to concrete directories/files (e.g., user/system-services/system-servers/tmpfs/CMakeLists.txt, kernel/sched/CMakeLists.txt, kernel/ipc/CMakeLists.txt, user/chcore-libc/CMakeLists.txt). However, it does not tie symptoms to the actual Lab3 grading tests (make grade) or mention the known runtime bug (use-after-free in sys_handle_mprotect).
IV. Bug Report Quality (20 pts).root_cause_analysis: 2/8 — Mostly attributes failures to a 'missing/incomplete distribution' and missing toolchain, without analyzing the actual underlying bug patterns expected in Lab3. The known CMake bug is mischaracterized (real issue is tmpfs target missing tmpfs.c in SOURCES, not that .c files are absent), and there is no analysis of the known kernel use-after-free in kernel/object/memory.c:sys_handle_mprotect().
IV. Bug Report Quality (20 pts).fix_solution: 2/6 — Provides concrete actions (copying files from other labs, creating stubs, editing CMakeLists), but the fixes are largely inappropriate for a bug-fix lab: copying whole implementations from Lab4/Lab5 and stubbing kernel IPC changes semantics and is unlikely to be the intended/acceptable fix for make grade. Also does not propose the correct fixes for the known issues (add tmpfs.c to CMake SOURCES; reorder obj_put/flush_tlb in sys_handle_mprotect).
IV. Bug Report Quality (20 pts).overall_comment: The report is detailed about perceived missing files and environment setup, but it misses the core Lab3 bug-fixing targets: it misidentifies the tmpfs CMake problem and does not cover the sys_handle_mprotect use-after-free. Proposed fixes rely on copying large amounts of code/stubbing, which is unlikely to be the intended minimal corrective patch set to pass make grade.
